rule: hit iff kw>=2 OR sem>=0.7 OR func>=0.7; total_hits>=1 -> FAIL

results.rationale: Source-domain PNAS paper on Macrotermes mound diurnal ventilation. Single source-side content-word match ('macrotermes mound ventilation'); no LLM mechanism, no LR schedule, no transformer artefact. Far below all thresholds.

Decoupled Relative Learning Rate Schedules (2025/07): assigns distinct cosine LR schedules to different model components vs a base model. This is the direct functional twin of TERMITE-VENT's two-channel cosine LR over a partitioned parameter set. Both primitive (per-component differential cosine LR) and artefact (transformer training LR schedule) coincide. Strongest match; meets both 0.7 thresholds.

WSD is the de facto modern LLM LR schedule, but it is single-channel, non-cyclic, and not phase-offset. Same artefact (LR schedule for transformer) but a different primitive. Just below both 0.7 thresholds.

Layer-wise Learning Rate Decay (LLRD) explicitly applies different LRs to shallow vs deep layers — the same depth-partitioned differential-LR primitive that TERMITE-VENT names 'two thermal-mass groups'. Same artefact, same parameter-partition primitive; only the cyclic phase-offset overlay is novel. Meets both 0.7 thresholds.

Periodic Behavior of NN Training with BN+WD characterises an emergent periodic instability arising from BN/WD interaction; it is descriptive, not a two-channel scheduling mechanism. Adjacent (periodic dynamics in training) but not the same artefact. Below thresholds.

SGDR cosine warm restarts: same artefact (cyclic cosine LR schedule). Operation differs (single global channel with growing period vs two phase-offset channels), but the cyclic-cosine-LR primitive is identical. Meets the 0.7 functional threshold.
AdamW decouples weight decay from the gradient update. Different mechanism axis (decay vs LR scheduling); not a parameter-partition or multi-channel-LR primitive. Below thresholds.
Peri-LN places layer norm peripherally to constrain residual-stream spikes. Different artefact (normalisation placement, not LR schedule) and different primitive. Well below thresholds.

fail_reason: 3 of 8 results trigger the hit rule. Decoupled Relative LR Schedules (arXiv 2507.03526) is a direct functional twin: distinct cosine LR per model component vs a base model is exactly TERMITE-VENT's two-channel cosine LR over partitioned parameter groups (sem=0.82, func=0.85). Layer-wise LR Decay supplies the depth-partitioned differential-LR primitive (the 'two thermal-mass groups' rename), and SGDR warm restarts supplies the cyclic-cosine-LR primitive. The phase-offset between two cyclic cosine channels is a positional refinement over densely-published differential-LR + cyclic-LR scheduling literature, not a novel mechanism. total_hits=3 >= 1 -> FAIL.
